entries.content:
It’s good that nothing much happened at this level—level one is the gentle tickle, the suggestion of threat rather than the bite, the domestic equivalent of rattling the cutlery drawer. In these little controlled agonies, we rehearse obedience; the rules say to obey, so obey we do, with trembling eyelids and a forced, pleasant smile. But let’s remember: even the smallest shock can curdle the milk or leave a crack in the porcelain—as the polite façade holds, panic thrums beneath the apron.
